title: Divide by 1-digit numbers with area models
description: Practice breaking up big division problems into smaller, simpler problems.
topic_path: Divide by 1-digit numbers with area models

assessment_items.question_content:
Lio split up the area of a rectangle to help him solve the equation $3{,}922 \div 3=?$.

He splits the rectangle into parts that are easy to divide by $3$ and a remainder.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the equations.**

 ||
-:| :- |:-
$a=$ | $1{,}000$ units|
$b=$ | [[☃ numeric-input 8]] units|
$c=$ |  $7$ units|
$h =$ |  $a+b+c=$ | [[☃ numeric-input 10]] units
$3{,}922 \div 3 =$ |[[☃ numeric-input 11]]| remainder [[☃ numeric-input 12]]
hints:
![same model emphasizing bottom part with a width of 3 units, an unlabeled height, and an area of 1 square unit](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/302a512ffd378ce1c2ba06276edb16f57961736c)

The last $\goldE{1}$ square unit is a remainder, because $1$ is less than $3$.
Now we know $h$ and the remainder. So we also know the answer to $3{,}922 \div 3$.

The correct answers:

$\begin{align}
&a = 1{,}000\\\\
&b = 300\\\\
&c = 7\\\\
&h = 1{,}307\\\\
&3{,}922 \div 3 = 1{,}307 \text{ remainder }1
\end{align}$

options.alt: Area model split into 4 parts, each with a width of 3 units. From top to bottom the parts have a height of A units and an area of 3,000 square units; a height of B units and an area of 900 square units; a height of C units and an area of 21 square units; an unlabeled height and an area of 1 square unit. A brace labeled H spans the top 3 parts.
